text.title: Agentic FinOps Workflow with AI Agents Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every destructive action (delete, terminate, release) MUST emit an approval request to the registered approver and wait for explicit ACK before execution. No silent auto-deletes.
rule.rationale: Autonomous deletes erode trust irreversibly; one wrong delete justifies regulating future agents.

rule.statement: Each agent runs under a dedicated IAM role with read-only by default; mutate permissions are conditional on approval-token presence (CloudTrail-verifiable).
rule.rationale: Broad agent IAM = lateral blast radius; scoped roles cap blast to the approved action.

rule.statement: Approval requests MUST include resource_id, action, reason, evidence_link (billing chart), estimated_savings, expiry_window, and a revoke_link.
rule.rationale: Vague 'approve?' messages cause rubber-stamping; structured payloads force evidence-based decisions.

rule.statement: Agents MUST enforce a per-hour and per-day action budget per environment; exceeding the budget pauses the agent and pings the on-call.
rule.rationale: Runaway agents (loop bug, mis-classification) are detected by the budget gate before they cause large-scale harm.

rule.statement: Each action MUST be logged to an append-only store with input snapshot, decision rationale, approval reference, and post-action verification.
rule.rationale: Without an evidence trail, postmortems devolve into he-said-she-said; auditability is non-negotiable for finance.

rule.statement: Skip when (a) FinOps INFORM phase incomplete OR (b) no approver registry OR (c) cloud spend below the threshold that justifies agent ops (<$10k/month).
rule.rationale: Below threshold the agent's own infra cost exceeds the savings; missing INFORM means agent runs on noisy data.
